/backend/api/v2/TrackProfile.trackDesc: Jyllands-Ringen is a motor racing circuit in Resenbro, near Silkeborg in Denmark. The circuit was extended from 1.475 kilometers to 2.3 kilometers in 2003, by linking the track to an adjacent oval circuit. The short, tight and twisty circuit was the home of the so-called Danish Grand Prix in 1968, 1969 and 1970 for Sports Prototypes and Sports GT cars. The rise of popularity of tin-top racing at the end of the 1990s saw the Jyllandsringen a center of activities for the new Danish Touring Car Championship (DTC).
